title: T-046 — ROOT: D365 integration UI group (Build modal + 8-step Wizard modal + SSE)
prompt:
# T-046 — D365 integration UI group (root/workflow descriptor)

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §10.6, §10.6.1 + UX d365_build_modal + d365_wizard_modal
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/modals.jsx (d365_build_modal 194-241, d365_wizard_modal 431-594)

## Goal (root)
This is a ROOT/WORKFLOW descriptor — it is NOT executable on its own. It describes the D365 integration UI group: single-click Build modal and 8-step Guided Wizard modal with SSE progress, launched from FA detail. It enumerates the atomic slice tasks that implement each modal/half plus wiring and parity tasks.

## Shared route
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/d365-build-modal.tsx` — single-click + MFA build modal
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/d365-wizard-modal-steps-1-4.tsx` — wizard steps 1-4 (validation, BOM, allergen, constants)
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/d365-wizard-modal-steps-5-8.tsx` — wizard steps 5-8 + SSE execute stream
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/fa/d365-progress-stream/route.ts` — SSE route streaming D365 build phases
- Both modals open from FA detail; the wizard reuses MFA validation hook from the Build modal.

## Slices under this root
- **T-123** — D365 Build modal (`d365_build_modal`, modals.jsx:194-241): single-click Build, MFA prompt, async SSE progress, BOM validation summary
- **T-124** — D365 Wizard steps 1-4 (`d365_wizard_modal`, modals.jsx:431-512, first half): wizard scaffold + step navigation (back/next), steps 1-4 (validate, bom_preview, allergen_check, constants_check) with per-step validation
- **T-125** — D365 Wizard steps 5-8 + SSE (`d365_wizard_modal`, modals.jsx:512-594, second half): steps 5-8 (n1_preview, mfa, execute_compute/xlsx/persist/emit) + EventSource client + dismissible=false guard during execute

## Wiring & parity
- **T-126** — WIRING: wire both modals into FA detail; Build button in FA opens D365BuildModal, wizard launches from modal; SSE route connects
- **T-127** — PARITY: Playwright screenshot parity vs modals.jsx for both modals; SSE events visible in network tab; 0 axe violations

## How to use this root
Do not implement code from this task. Dispatch slices T-123 (Build modal) and T-124 (Wizard 1-4) in parallel; T-125 (Wizard 5-8 + SSE) depends on T-124. Then T-126 (wiring), then T-127 (parity).

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement any modal UI in this root task
- Do not allow dismiss during execute phase (enforced in T-125)
- Do not invoke buildD365 directly from client during wizard (must be wizardExecute)
- Do not render plain HTML <input> for MFA (use shadcn Input)

pipeline_inputs.description: Root/workflow descriptor for the D365 integration UI group. Enumerates 3 slice tasks (T-123 Build, T-124 Wizard 1-4, T-125 Wizard 5-8+SSE) + wiring (T-126) + parity (T-127).
pipeline_inputs.details: ROOT task — not executable. Describes Build modal, 8-step Wizard split into 1-4 / 5-8+SSE halves, SSE route, and FA wiring. Phases: validate, bom_preview, allergen_check, constants_check, n1_preview, mfa, execute_compute, execute_xlsx, execute_persist, execute_emit.

acceptance_criteria: This task is a root/workflow descriptor and is closed when all child slices T-123..T-127 are closed with their own ACs satisfied.
Both modals (Build, 8-step Wizard) render and are reachable from FA detail with working SSE — verified by T-126 wiring task.
Browser screenshot parity vs modals.jsx is recorded by T-127 parity task with 0 axe violations and SSE events observed.
test_strategy: No tests at root level — each slice (T-123, T-124, T-125) has its own RTL tests; T-126 has wiring tests; T-127 runs Playwright parity.
